step_by_step_thinking:
Vimentin is a component of the extracellular matrix and interacts with actin stress fibers, making it part of the cytoskeleton. Keratin is indeed a component of intermediate filaments specifically in epithelial cells. Desmin is another type of intermediate filament found in smooth and skeletal muscle cells. Tubulin, however, belongs to the microtubule family rather than intermediate filaments. Therefore:
\boxed{D} Based on the information presented, tubulin does not belong to the category of intermediate filaments but instead pertains to microtubules, which are separate components of the cytoskeleton alongside actin filaments and intermediate filaments. Thus, D is correct.
